name: Mutability
description: Questions on mutable vs immutable types and side effects.

questions.question: Which of these Python types is mutable?
questions.explanation: Dicts are mutable; str, tuple, and frozenset are immutable.

questions.question: Passing a list to a function and appending inside the function:
choices: Does not change the original list
questions.explanation: Lists are mutable; the function receives a reference, so mutating it affects the caller's list.

questions.question: Which is hashable?
questions.explanation: Hashable types are immutable. A tuple of ints is hashable; list, set, and dict are not.

questions.question: a = [1,2]; b = a; b.append(3). What is a?
questions.explanation: a and b refer to the same list; appending to b mutates the same object as a.

questions.question: To avoid mutating the original list you can:
questions.explanation: lst[:] or list(lst) creates a shallow copy so modifications don't affect the original.
